campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met on September 2, 2025 to review the city's finances for July, including how the city invested its money and spent its budget. The council also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, like biking, walking, and public transit routes.
keyTopics: July 2025 financial report and investment account activity
City budget and spending overview for July
Campbell Multimodal Transportation Plan - improving biking, walking, and transit options
Implementation roadmap and timeline for transportation improvements

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public meeting on March 18, 2026 where residents had a chance to speak about a new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council introduced a new ordinance (a city rule) and decided to fast-track it by skipping the normal first reading process so they can vote to officially adopt it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and expedited for faster approval
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption vote at next council meeting
First reading waived to speed up the process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from several March sessions and authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda was mostly administrative housekeeping items reviewing past meeting records.
keyTopics: Approved multiple sets of meeting minutes from March 2026
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Routine administrative approval items

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize upcoming agendas for April 21 and April 28, 2026. They reviewed what items should be added to each agenda, set specific times for certain discussions, and checked whether any documents needed special approval notices. The council also reviewed public records from early April and discussed VEBA (a benefits program for city employees).
keyTopics: Finalized April 21 council meeting agenda and added new items
Organized April 28 draft agenda with scheduling adjustments
Reviewed public records from April 2-9, 2026
VEBA Advisory Committee discussion
Document waiver requirements for agenda items

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to review the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, focusing on major construction and infrastructure projects. The council also formally recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: Next year's budget for building projects and city improvements
Teen Month recognition and celebration
National Volunteer Week proclamation
Planning session for capital infrastructure spending

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review their previous month's notes and discuss changes to tenant protection rules in Sunnyvale. The main focus was introducing a new ordinance that would update the city's policies on how renters are treated and protected.
keyTopics: Approval of March 2026 meeting minutes
New ordinance to update residential tenant protection rules
Changes to Chapter 19.71 of Sunnyvale Municipal Code

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 16, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 16, 2026 where residents had the opportunity to speak about issues affecting the city. The council also held elections to choose new leadership positions and discussed next year's budget planning.
keyTopics: Public comment period - residents could speak for up to 3 minutes on city matters
Election of new Chair and Vice Chair leaders
Fiscal year budget development and planning

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 covered important legal rules that residents should know about. If you disagree with a city decision and want to take legal action, you have 90 days from when the decision is finalized to file a lawsuit—after that deadline, you lose your right to challenge it in court.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to sue the city over decisions
Legal challenges to city decisions must follow state law (Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6)
Importance of acting quickly if you want to contest a city ruling

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to discuss updates to the city's federal funding plan for 2025-2030, review new project requests from individual council members, and consider changing the rules for how council vacancies are filled. The council also approved meeting minutes and previewed upcoming agenda items.
keyTopics: Updates to federal funding plan (Consolidated Plan) for housing and community development
New project requests submitted by individual council members
Changes to council vacancy procedures and rules
City Council meeting schedule and upcoming agenda items

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner wants to officially recognize it as a historic building and get permission to add 5,000 square feet of extra space as part of renovations to improve the building's safety and preserve its historic character.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic designation upgrade
5,000 sq ft bonus floor area for renovation project
Seismic safety improvements to historic building
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule updates
March 12, 2026 meeting minutes approval
